title: RFC: use shfmt for bash/bats/sh files
body:
This is big and therefore controversial, but I still think it brings more good than harm.

Let's shfmt everything!
    
The main idea here is to unify shell/bash/bats formatting, relying
on a tool to do that (similar to how it is done for Go).
    
Default shfmt rules (except for using 4 spaces for indentation, which
seems to be the de facto standard in this repo) were deliberately
chosen not to complicate things further (adding `--case-indent` and
`--space-redirects` would make the patch smaller, but not by much).
    
Generated by shfmt v3.11.0 using this command:
```
shfmt -w .
```

As (mostly) a side effect, this adds `.editorconfig` which makes my
nvim to honor the "use 4 spaces in shell files".

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
NONE
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *kolyshkin* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [mtrmac](https://github.com/mtrmac) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.

I am not sure how I feel about this, on the one hand yes I like better more uniform style and especially tabs vs spaces mixes drives me nuts.

But this also changes a lot more, and while from a quick look nothing seems wrong I don't believe that most changes are really any better from this. While I personally would be fine with a shell linter I think it also raises the random contributor makes PR and then gets lint failure and then they don't bother and go away.


Also your `.editorconfig: add` is missing an actual commit reference, I suppose https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/86e55d0ec1acd79f70bd2d41f02f44a90c35deb7 would be a good candidate to cite.

> While I personally would be fine with a shell linter I think it also raises the random contributor makes PR and then gets lint failure and then they don't bother and go away.

It's not a linter (that would be `shellcheck` and it's much more work), it's merely a formatter, and a way to fix this when a CI will fail is to call `make shfmt`.

> But this also changes a lot more, and while from a quick look nothing seems wrong I don't believe that most changes are really any better from this.

I think it is better, for the most part. It mostly adds missing (or removes extra) spaces and newlines. The added/removed spaces is mostly for the good, except one particular case, where it removes a space in between a redirection operator (such as `>`, `>&`, or `<<<`) and a file name. This is something I don't like personally but it's a preference (and can be changed). Example:

```diff
-$(grep -E -B 5 -A 5 "$regex"<<<"$diffs")"
+$(grep -E -B 5 -A 5 "$regex" <<<"$diffs")"
```

Other than that, it:

1. Removes `\` and `;` at EOL where it is not necessary, for example:

```diff
-cat "$ID_NAME_FILEPATH" | \
+cat "$ID_NAME_FILEPATH" |
```
```diff
-            continue;
+            continue
```

A variation of this is:
```diff
-git branch -r | grep /dependabot/go_modules/ \
-    | xargs --no-run-if-empty git branch -r -d
+git branch -r | grep /dependabot/go_modules/ |
+    xargs --no-run-if-empty git branch -r -d
```

2. Replaces backticks with `$()`:

```diff
-    value=`expr "$i" : '[^=]*=\(.*\)'`
+    value=$(expr "$i" : '[^=]*=\(.*\)')
```

3. Puts `then` and `do` on the same line (technically it's same as removing extra newlines when adding missing `;`):

```diff
-    for unit in podman.socket podman-auto-update.timer
-    do
-        for state in enabled active
-        do
-            if systemctl --quiet is-$state $unit
-            then
+    for unit in podman.socket podman-auto-update.timer; do
+        for state in enabled active; do
+            if systemctl --quiet is-$state $unit; then
```

4. Adds missing `;;` after the last switch case (this seeems redundant but good for uniformity):
```diff
-    *) die_unknown TEST_ENVIRON
+*) die_unknown TEST_ENVIRON ;;
 esac
```

To me all of that (space/newline addition and removal, as well as 1, 2, 3, 4 above) looks good and beneficial.

Perhaps you can review the commit with `--ignore-all-space` (or a similar option on github) again to re-assess?

> Also your `.editorconfig: add` is missing an actual commit reference, I suppose [86e55d0](https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/86e55d0ec1acd79f70bd2d41f02f44a90c35deb7) would be a good candidate to cite.

Yes, this is exactly what I meant to add :) Fixed.

Yeah not a linter, which makes it worse since it doesn't catch any real issue so we gain "readability" only.
Each extra step someone has to do to contribute can be annoying, it requires often force pushes even for maintainers to fix things up, etc... The whitespace issues here add IMO no value at all and make things worse since they bloat the diff.
The redirect whitespace changes are just pointless to me, they are not easier to read just because it removed the space there, it is not about me ignoring the whitespaces in the diff it is about making pointless changes that don't improve the format for me `command > /file` and `command >/file` are both perfectly fine to read and so I just don't like this change.

Consistency is great in general and I l like the indentation changes as this clearly helps me when reading code (i.e. when I try skip ahead). But a ton of random scripts here that really do not benefit from this at all. For tests I think it could be useful to have a proper style but even there the rules should not be so super strict.

> The redirect whitespace changes are just pointless to me, they are not easier to read just because it removed the space there, it is not about me ignoring the whitespaces in the diff it is about making pointless changes that don't improve the format for me command > /file and command >/file are both perfectly fine to read and so I just don't like this change.

As I said earlier, it's a preference and can be changed. Alas, what can't be changed (in shfmt) is to allow both ways (with and without the space).

> For tests I think it could be useful to have a proper style but even there the rules should not be so super strict.

I've tried it for tests only, and with "space redirects" option enabled. It's still a big diff (`84 files changed, 2249 insertions(+), 2194 deletions(-)`), so let me just close this.
